text: The ever-present challenge of synthesizing helical structures such as carbohelicenes in the solid state and their numerous applications have fascinated generations of chemists. The strong CPL emission that can be obtained from these molecules has triggered a renewed interest in these types of materials for potential applications in materials science. The enantioselective synthesis of the fused carbon/hetero-helicenes was achieved several years ago. However, to the best of our knowledge, there is not yet any reported catalytic enantioselective synthesis of lower non-fused carbo[ n ]helicenes ( n = 4–6). In this article we report on the Pd catalysed enantioselective C–H arylation using a bifunctional bifurcate P-OC ligand and show that this method gives access to any of the lower carbo[ n ]helicenes in good yields and enantioselectivity. Modelling studies indicate that the enantioselectivity is the result of a balance between activation energy for the C–H activation and reductive elimination steps. The enantiodetermination of the [ n ]helicene comes from the competition between non-covalent interactions between the substrate and the ligand as well as from steric repulsions during the two reductive elimination steps of the catalytic cycle. Their photophysical and chiroptical properties have been examined in detail over a wide range of concentrations to quantify their potential application to luminescent materials with circularly polarised light emission.
